skill.name: analyze-project
skill.description: Rigor Analyze / Rigor Audit read-only skill for deep learning research repositories. Use when the user wants to read and understand a repository, inspect model structure and training or inference entrypoints, review configs and insertion points, or flag suspicious implementation patterns without modifying code or running heavy jobs. Do not use for active command execution, broad refactoring, speculative code adaptation, or automatic bug fixing.
skill.summary: Read-only static analysis skill for mapping deep learning repository structure, entrypoints, configs, and suspicious ML implementation patterns.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged 32 potential security issues, but all are false positives after semantic evaluation. The script uses ast.parse for safe AST-based static analysis only, does not execute arbitrary code, and has no network access. Flagged 'eval()' patterns are PyTorch model evaluation mode (.eval()), not code evaluation. 'Backtick' patterns in SKILL.md are markdown formatting. 'Weak crypto' and 'path traversal' flags are regex matches on documentation text, not actual dangerous code. The skill is genuinely read-only as documented.

low_findings.title: Static analysis pattern matches on legitimate code
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 'eval()' in scripts/analyze_project.py:313, but this is a string check for '.eval()' in PyTorch model code (checking if models are in evaluation mode), not Python's eval() function. The script uses ast.parse for safe parsing and has no code execution capabilities.

low_findings.title: Backtick patterns are markdown formatting
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged backtick characters in SKILL.md and scripts/analyze_project.py as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution', but these are standard markdown code formatting (e.g., `code`) and Python f-string formatting, not shell command execution. No subprocess calls exist in the codebase.

content.value_statement: Understanding a new deep learning repository is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill provides read-only static analysis to map model structure, training entrypoints, and suspicious patterns without modifying code or running expensive training jobs.
actual_capabilities: Maps training, inference, evaluation, model, and config entrypoints in deep learning repositories
Identifies task-relevant files using pattern matching on filenames and content
Detects suspicious ML implementation patterns (activation misuse, dropout in eval mode, missing position encodings)
Generates structured Markdown reports (SUMMARY, RISKS, RESEARCH_MAP, CHANGE_MAP, EVAL_CONTRACT)
Performs read-only static analysis using Python's ast module for safe code parsing
Excludes cache, build, and output directories from analysis to focus on source code
limitations: Read-only by design - cannot modify, patch, or run code in the target repository
Does not execute training or evaluation jobs - only analyzes code structure
Suspicious pattern detection uses heuristics, not confirmed bug identification
Requires Python 3 with PyYAML available for YAML context file support
Limited to filesystem-based analysis - no network access or remote repository support

use_cases.title: Onboarding to a new ML codebase
use_cases.description: Quickly understand the structure of an unfamiliar deep learning repository before making changes. Identify where models are defined, how training is launched, and what configs control behavior.
use_cases.target_user: ML engineers joining a new project

use_cases.title: Pre-refactor repository audit
use_cases.description: Map protected evaluation zones and high-risk change areas before refactoring. Understand which files are critical to evaluation correctness and which are safe to modify.

use_cases.title: Code review preparation
use_cases.description: Generate a structured summary of a research repository for code review meetings. Highlight suspicious patterns and unclear architecture decisions for discussion.

prompt_templates.title: Basic repository overview
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this deep learning project and give me an overview of the main components, training entrypoints, and model structure.
prompt_templates.scenario: First-time exploration of a new repository

prompt_templates.title: Identify safe modification zones
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this repository and tell me which files I can safely modify for a feature addition, and which are protected evaluation zones I should not touch.

prompt_templates.title: Review suspicious patterns
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the analyze-project skill on this repo and report any suspicious implementation patterns. Flag anything that looks like a potential bug or anti-pattern.

prompt_templates.title: Generate research map for documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this research repository and produce a RESEARCH_MAP.md that documents the model architecture, training pipeline, and config relationships for our internal wiki.

output: - Read the main model file before changing configs
- Verify train entrypoint before inserting modules

output_examples.input: What suspicious patterns exist in this repository?
output: - models/attention.py: review whether dropout-sensitive evaluation behavior is intentional
- train.py: verify optimizer parameter coverage if custom freezing is expected
- Repository contains attention-like code but no obvious positional encoding signal was detected

best_practices: Run the analysis before making any code changes to understand the repository structure
Treat suspicious pattern findings as heuristics requiring human verification, not confirmed bugs
Use the generated CHANGE_MAP.md to identify protected evaluation zones before refactoring
Combine this skill with targeted manual code review for critical sections
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to patch or modify repository code - it is read-only by design
Do not treat heuristic pattern matches as confirmed bugs without further investigation
Do not run heavy training or evaluation jobs expecting this skill to handle them
Do not use this skill for general literature summaries or non-code research tasks

faq.question: Is this skill safe to run on my codebase?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is read-only and only performs static analysis using Python's ast module. It does not execute code, make network requests, or modify any files in the target repository.

faq.question: What output files does the analysis produce?
faq.answer: The analysis writes SUMMARY.md, RISKS.md, RESEARCH_MAP.md, CHANGE_MAP.md, EVAL_CONTRACT.md, and status.json to the analysis_outputs directory.

faq.question: Can this skill run training or evaluation jobs?
faq.answer: No. This skill is explicitly read-only. It analyzes code structure and patterns but does not execute any commands or run training jobs.

faq.question: What ML frameworks does this support?
faq.answer: The skill is framework-agnostic. It uses filename and content pattern matching to identify deep learning components, working with PyTorch, TensorFlow, JAX, and other frameworks.

faq.question: How accurate are the suspicious pattern detections?
faq.answer: Pattern detections are heuristics, not confirmed bug identifications. The skill marks findings as unverified and recommends human review before acting on them.

faq.question: Can I customize what files are analyzed?
faq.answer: The skill skips common output and cache directories by default. You can pass an analysis context JSON or YAML file to specify task family and evaluation source for more targeted analysis.
